decision: Morgan D-CFO should be deployed to Microsoft Foundry as a hosted agent for the real runtime, with App Service remaining as the web/voice/showcase companion surface.
preparedArtifacts: Dockerfile updated to support builds without package-lock.json and start dist/foundryHost.js.
src/foundryHost.ts added as the dedicated Foundry Responses host.
src/foundry/responsesAdapter.ts exposes GET /readiness, GET /responses/health, and POST /responses.
scripts/prepare-foundry-hosted-agent-definition.cjs excludes reserved FOUNDRY_*, PORT, and AGENT_* variables and defaults smoke payloads to responses-only mode.
scripts/diagnose-foundry-hosted-readiness.cjs validates the Foundry host and readiness route.
.foundry/agent-metadata.yaml records northCentralHosted as the default environment.
verifiedByExecution: Integrated Cloud Shell npm run build completed after adding src/foundryHost.ts.
Cloud Shell local production probe returned HTTP 200 from /readiness on port 8088.
Cloud Shell local POST /responses returned Morgan setup-verification output.
ACR build/push succeeded for crbdoregvn6di7y.azurecr.io/morgan-digital-cfo:20260507193551 with run ID cp4.
Foundry agent_invoke succeeded with request ID a3e93a531857d3293afb0a72b1147247.
gpt-5-mini deployment succeeded in the North Central US Foundry account.
Hosted instance and blueprint identities were assigned Azure AI User and Cognitive Services OpenAI User at the account scope.
Foundry agent_get reported version 7 active with AZURE_CLIENT_ID set to the hosted instance identity.
Direct REST to the hosted Responses endpoint succeeded for version 7 with response ID resp_1778184291367 and correlation ID 26661326-b246-4d31-b296-ea6f3e815d91.
ACR build/push succeeded for crbdoregvn6di7y.azurecr.io/morgan-digital-cfo:20260507204912 with run ID cp7.
Foundry agent_get reported version 10 active with pre-showcase readiness guard and demo-label smoke guard metadata.
Direct REST P0 smoke passed for version 10 with response IDs resp_1778187198884, resp_1778187274037, resp_1778187350105, and resp_1778187425350.

deploymentAttempts.reason: session_not_ready before /readiness fix and dedicated Foundry host.
session_not_ready after /readiness, before production default port and dedicated Foundry host.
session_not_ready; local reproduction showed Microsoft Agents SDK adapter required ClientId at startup.
Dedicated Foundry Responses host started on 8088 and answered /responses.
Live Azure OpenAI env was present, but hosted identity RBAC and AZURE_CLIENT_ID were not yet configured. Correlation ID 6ac551ad597271addf4371edccf5ec26.
RBAC was assigned, but AZURE_CLIENT_ID was still missing for the user-assigned hosted identity. Correlation ID b4c36c0192360ac1e28af57c2849e80c.
AZURE_CLIENT_ID was set and the direct Foundry hosted Responses endpoint completed with gpt-5-mini. MCP agent_invoke helper still failed with correlation ID 7b8952ae6f51f8862dcb2181b7b7f811.
Hosted readiness/configuration guard was added, but showcase shortcuts still intercepted Microsoft IQ settings prompts first.
Readiness/configuration guard moved before showcase shortcuts; settings prompt passed, but end-of-day demo prompt still needed an explicit deterministic context label.
Pre-showcase readiness guard plus explicit demo/deterministic end-of-day label made all four P0 smoke prompts complete and pass content gates.

currentSmokeOutput: Morgan is running as a hosted-agent container, but AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT is not configured. Mission Control, health, and audit endpoints are available for setup verification.
currentLiveOutput: Direct REST returned a completed gpt-5-mini response for session morganhostedv7rest000001x. The model text mentioned Agent 365 MCP endpoints, but connector proof remains unconfigured.
remainingGates: Use direct REST or azd invoke for vNext hosted Responses proof while MCP agent_invoke is returning internal helper errors.
Review Graph/MCP, ACS/Teams voice, observability, and storage values before adding them to a hosted-agent version.
Run managed Foundry batch evaluation and live connector verification before claiming production parity.
